slices.focus: Corridor scoreboard to defend concentrated strategy with data
slices.summary: Built a block-level corridor scoreboard tracking completed applications, signed scopes, and visible project starts per block to shift the narrative from proving effort to proving outcomes. The spatial momentum data successfully convinced leadership to maintain the concentrated strategy. Keeping the front page spare with trend markers and an appendix for definitions. Both strong and weak blocks shown together to maintain honest communication.
open_loops: How to politely decline other departments' requests to add columns and agendas to the scoreboard
Whether a single month of weak data would trigger premature calls to abandon the concentrated approach
How to protect the tool from being mistaken for a countywide performance dashboard rather than a project-specific tool
decisions: Keep the scoreboard front page spare; add directional trend markers and an optional backup appendix
Show both strong and weak blocks together, framing weaker pockets as next-step management opportunities
Add an intended-use sentence to prevent scope creep from other departments wanting to add metrics
